_comment: Stage 9e Inner arc miter — Steps 1-5 of doc/inner_arc_miter_plan.md implemented: (1) reposition non-BEV-BEV BVs along bev edge; (2-3) (seg+1)² patch grid + seg² patch quads via materializeArcMiterPatch (quarter-circle inner/outer arcs + bilinear interior); (4) splice patch boundary into the 3 surrounding faces (front-cap, inner-side, BEV-BEV inner-bottom); (5) override non-BEV-BEV BVs' cap-profile sampleVertIds + sample positions to point into the patch grid so strip emission weaves directly into the patch as bridging quads, and suppress the legacy cap polygon + leftBV cap-mid allocation. Result: vibe3d 25v/16f vs Blender 24v/16f, fv-distribution matches exactly {4:11, 6:2, 7:1, 5:1, 8:1}. 22 of 24 Blender verts match exactly (4 corners + inner/outer arc midpoints + side_a0 linear midpoint + 14 surrounding-face/strip verts); 2 close-but-not-exact (within 0.03 tol): the patch center and side_aN_mid (= a=N side mid) — both bilinear approximations to Blender's iterative-CC interior. The single remaining vibe3d-extra vert is the BEV-BEV BoundVert (reuses bv.vert via reusesOrig=true), now an orphan after the face splicing routes around it; removing it would require mesh-vertex compaction (no removeVertex API). Topologically equivalent to Blender mod the orphan — the case stays XFAIL only because of this orphan + the 2 sub-tolerance approximations.
